DECLARATION OF UJVALA SINGH IN SUPPORT OF MOTION (1) TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION PROPOUNDED TO PLAINTIFFS; (2) TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND (3) FOR SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 15011138) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROBERT CROSS AN INDIVIDUAL SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-11-2015 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN AND MICHAEL LEVINE'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED, BUT FOR THOSE AS TO PRIVILEGES AND PRIVACY. PLAINTIFFS BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN AND MICHAEL LEVINE SHALL GIVE VERIFIED AND OTHERWISE FULLY CODE-COMPLIANT FURTHER RESPONSES, AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, WITHOUT FURTHER OBJECTIONS, TO ALL SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION ENUMERATED IN THE MOTION WITHIN TEN CALENDAR DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE ORDER, EXCEPT AS TO THE REMAINING OBJECTIONS. ANY RESPONSE OR PRODUCTION AS TO WHICH PRIVILEGE AND/OR PRIVACY OBJECTIONS ARE ASSERTED MUST BE SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE/PRIVACY LOG WITH REFERENCE TO THE SPECIFIC INTERROGATORY OR REQUEST UNDER WHICH IT FALLS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,585.00 ARE AWARDED DEFENDANT SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP AGAINST DIMITRIOU & ASSOCIATES, PC, AND ARE PAYABLE WITHIN TEN CALENDAR DAYS OF THE SERVICE OF THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: DENNIS HYDE; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

DECLARATION OF GUY D. CALLADINE IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION (1) FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS; AND (2) FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 15075187) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROBERT CROSS AN INDIVIDUAL SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP

LAW AND MOTION, 302, AS TO DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S MOTION (1) FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS; AND (2) FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS ARE AWARDED $2500 IN SANCTIONS PAYABLE NO LATER THAN MAY 15, 2015. IF PLAINTIFFS DO NOT PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AS REQUIRED BY THE FEBRUARY 11, 2015 ORDER ON OR BEFORE MAY 15, 2015, THEN DEFENDANTS MAY MAKE AN EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IN DEPARTMENT 613 WITH PROOF OF PLAINTIFFS' FAILURE TO SO COMPLY AND, UPON PRESENTATION OF SUCH PROOF AND THE ABSENCE OF A SATISFACTORY EXCUSE, TERMINATING SANCTIONS WILL BE GRANTED AGAINST PLAINTIFFS. THE REDUCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF THE SANCTIONS REQUESTED REFLECTS THE FACT THAT THIS WAS A VERY SIMPLE MOTION THAT COULD HAVE BEEN FILED AND HEARD WITH ONLY A FEW HOURS OF WORK BY DEFENSE COUNSEL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR NO. 5963 (302/HK)

DISCOVERY. 302, DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS, ISSUE SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL L. LEVINE SHALL APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 11, 2015. SHOULD PLAINTIFF MICHAEL L. LEVINE FAIL TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 11, 2015, THE COURT WILL, UPON PROOF OF PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO APPEAR, CONSIDER EVIDENTIARY, TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,500. COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH THE PENDING DEPOSITION ARE NOT INCLUDED IN THE AWARD OF SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: REBECCA WOODSON, CLERK: SEAN KANE, REPORTER: CONNIE MCCARTHY, CSR# 13173, TEL NO. 415-572-1519. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions; Or In The Alternative For Evidentiary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW A. CEBRIAN, ESQ. of Jones & Devoy LLP, Tel No. 415-615-0879 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. UJVALA SINGH, ESQ. of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP, Tel No. 415-593-8240 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - Defense counsel stipulates to the pro tem judge. The Court determines that Plaintiff's notice of intent to contest the court's tentative ruling was not timely made. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions; Or In The Alternative For Evidentiary Sanctions, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff Michael L. Levine shall appear for deposition on or before September 11, 2015. Should Plaintiff Michael L. Levine fail to appear for deposition on or before September 11, 2015, the Court will, upon proof of plaintiff's failure to appear, consider evidentiary, terminating and monetary sanctions. Defendants' request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,500. Costs associated with the pending deposition are not included in the award of sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Rebecca Woodson, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Connie McCarthy, CSR# 13173, Tel No. 415-572-1519. (302/JPT)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion (1) For Terminating Sanctions; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ujvala Singh, of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP, Tel. 415.593.8240, for defendants;
No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS ROBERT CROSS AND SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP'S Motion (1) For Terminating Sanctions; And (2) For Monetary Sanctions. The Motion is Granted in part and denied in part. Defendants are awarded $2500 in sanctions payable no later than May 15, 2015. If plaintiffs do not provide further responses as required by the February 11, 2015 order on or before May 15, 2015, then defendants may make an ex parte application for terminating sanctions in department 613 with proof of plaintiffs' failure to so comply and, upon presentation of such proof and the absence of a satisfactory excuse, terminating sanctions will be granted against plaintiffs. The reduction in the amount of the sanctions requested reflects the fact that this was a very simple motion that could have been filed and heard with only a few hours of work by defense counsel. Order signed. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. UJVALA SINGH OF THE CARLSON, CALADINE & PETERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 593-8240 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS is granted. Plaintiffs BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN and MICHAEL LEVINE's objections are overruled, but for those as to privileges and privacy. Plaintiffs BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN and MICHAEL LEVINE shall give verified and otherwise fully code-compliant further responses, and produce documents, without further objections, to all special interrogatories and requests for production enumerated in the motion within ten calendar days of service of the order, except as to the remaining objections. Any response or production as to which privilege and/or privacy objections are asserted must be specifically identified in a privilege/privacy log with reference to the specific interrogatory or request under which it falls. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $6,585.00 are awarded Defendant SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP against Dimitriou & Associates, PC, and are payable within ten calendar days of the service of the order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro-tem: Dennis Hyde; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
